tweets.full_text:
AI開発エージェントを比較して分かったこと。

「一番賢いAI」を探すより、
役割分担した方が強い。

今回、同じLP制作を
Antigravity・Claude Code・Codex に任せて検証。

結果はこうでした。

品質：Codex
安定・設計：Claude Code
速度：Antigravity

つまり、
速いAIと、仕上げるAIは違う。

AIに全部任せると壊れる。
でも、役割を分けると“AI開発チーム”になる。

HSビルで実際に使っているCOO/CTO型AI組織の考え方を記事にまとめました。

https://t.co/cfhmRjhFjS

既に色々AIに任せてるけど、Claude Codeが優秀すぎて、普通に2〜3人で事業回せる未来見えてる。

例えばなんだけど、

quoted_status.full_text:
【Claude Code講座 アーカイブ公開】

832名が申し込んだ「非エンジニア向けClaude Code講座」終了後も多方面からアーカイブ希望の声をいただいたので、noteを作成しました。

▶︎ https://t.co/BjWLgtYGyn

当日参加できなかった方も、これからClaude Codeを学びたい方も、 ぜひこれを見て勉強してください。

特典も11個以上で過去一番コンテンツを出しました。

非エンジニアがClaude Codeを使いこなすのに必要な情報、全部入ってます。

━━━━━━━━━━━━
📌この講座で学べること
━━━━━━━━━━━━

・Claude Codeの本質（なぜウェブ版の10倍働けるのか）
・Cursor × Claude Code の最強環境
・CLAUDE.md設計（3層構造でAIが育つ）
・Skillsで業務を一発再現する方法
・MCP連携（Gmail・Slack・Notion等16サービス）
・Agent Teamで複数AIを同時起動
・フォルダ整理・議事録・Excel自動生成のハンズオン
・セキュリティ対策5選
・Q&A 132問（Slidoで殺到した180問から厳選）

━━━━━━━━━━━━
🎁11大特典付き
━━━━━━━━━━━━

① 事前質問Q&A集（821名分・匿名化済み）
② 投影資料（全スライド）
③ アーカイブ動画（約2時間40分）
④ チャエンのClaude Code運用ノウハウ
　▶︎スキル・MCP・コマンド・運用設計
⑤ セミナーレポート
⑥ Q&A 132問（Slido質疑応答）
⑦ バイブコーディング・マスターガイド
⑧ 30日勉強ロードマップ
⑨ 復習AI Bot（NotebookLM）
⑩ 事前学習動画（セットアップ解説）
⑪ 約10万文字のClaude Code書籍

参加者の99.4%が「不満なし」
NPS+37.0（業界平均の2倍以上）
71.8%が「応用編も受けたい」と回答。

本格的にClaude Codeを使いこなしたい方は、ぜひ。
期間限定割引もあるので、お早めにどうぞ。

▶https://t.co/BjWLgtYGyn

tweets.full_text:
【最新AIニュース】
Anthropicが、Claude CodeとClaude APIの利用上限引き上げを発表しました。

開発者向けニュースに見えますが、建設業・リフォーム業の社長にも関係あります。

ポイントは「AIがどれだけ長く、安定して働けるか」です。

何が変わったか：
・Claude Codeの5時間レート制限をPro/Max/Team/Enterpriseで倍増
・Pro/Maxのピーク時間帯制限を緩和
・Claude Opus系APIの上限を引き上げ
・SpaceXとの計算資源提携で処理能力を増強

建設業で効くのはここです：
1. 朝のメール・予定・未対応タスク整理
2. 現場写真や報告メモの文章化
3. 見積前の資料整理
4. 営業後フォロー文面の下書き
5. SNS・note・社内文書の定例作成

AI活用は「たまに質問する」段階から、毎日動く実務担当へ移っています。

ただし、送信・削除・金銭操作・契約・顧客情報の扱いは人間承認が必須。
完全自動化ではなく、調査・整理・下書きまでAI社員に任せ、最後の判断は社長が行う設計が現実的です。

社長のPC業務を減らすAI社員は、賢さだけでなく「利用上限・安定稼働・承認設計」まで見て育てるべきです。

詳しく整理しました：
https://t.co/aoUkZbz4KX

参照元：Anthropic公式
https://t.co/2XACimkmmr

quoted_status.full_text:
・Microsoftは社内で急速に普及したClaude Codeの利用を縮小し、GitHub Copilot CLIへ移行を進めている。
・背景には、社員によるAI利用量の急増でコストが膨らみすぎた問題がある。
・UberでもAIコーディング支援ツールの年間予算を、わずか4カ月で使い切ったという。
・MetaやAmazonも、社員に「もっとAIを使え」と競わせる文化を強めている。
・しかしAIは「使えば使うほど料金が増える」従量課金モデルであり、生産性向上とコスト増加が同時に進む。
・Gartnerは、2030年にはAIトークン単価が大幅に下がる一方、エージェントAIの利用増で総コストは逆に増える可能性を指摘している。
・NVIDIAの幹部も「計算資源コストは社員コストを大きく超えている」と認め始めている。

興味深いのは、「AIが安くなる」という話と、「AI導入コストが爆発する」という話が、同時に成立している点です。
半導体やクラウドの世界では昔から、単価が下がると利用量が爆発的に増え、結果として総消費量が拡大する現象がありました。AIでも同じことが起きつつあり、特にエージェント型AIは、一回のタスクで大量の推論を繰り返すため、単純なチャットAIとは比較にならないほどトークンを消費します。

https://t.co/OM3x4djTy7 

また、別の記事ではマイクロソフトは社内の意思決定をスリム化し、スタートアップのような企業を目指しているともあります。Microsoftの組織改革は「AI時代のコスト戦争」に備える動きにも見えてきます。AIは便利な新機能である一方、膨大なGPU・電力・推論コストを必要とする“重工業”でもある。だからこそMicrosoftは、巨大企業の遅い意思決定を壊し、より速く回収・改善できる組織へ変わろうとしているのかもしれません。

tweets.full_text:
MicrosoftがClaude Codeの大半を6月末で打ち切り
#AI #Microsoft #Claude

MicrosoftのExperiences + Devicesチーム（Windows、Microsoft 365、Teamsなどを担当）が、AnthropicのClaude Codeライセンスの大部分を2026年6月末までに終了すると報じられました。
理由は「利用しすぎてコストが膨張した」ことと、自社ツールであるGitHub Copilot CLIへの移行です。

人気が出すぎた結果、自社のAIエコシステムに影響が出始めた象徴的な動きと言えます。
トークン課金型の生成AIは、少人数利用では強力ですが、大規模展開では予算を急激に圧迫するケースが目立ってきています。
（情報元：Tom Warren氏 / The Verge）

この動きは、AIの実務導入が本格化する中で「生産性向上 vs コスト現実」のバランスが問われ始めたことを示しています。
Uberでも2026年AI予算を4月までに使い切った事例が報じられるなど、企業側の実態が少しずつ見えてきました👀

tweets.full_text:
Marc Andreessen admitted on Joe Rogan that AI is making people less efficient.

The guy who funds half the AI industry. On a podcast. Just casually dropping it.

Same week: Nvidia's VP said compute now costs more than his employees. 

Microsoft canceled 100,000 Claude Code licenses because finance couldn't stomach the bill. 

Uber burned $3.4 billion in AI budget by April.

And here's the detail everyone's glossing over:

Uber didn't just adopt AI. They gamified it. Internal leaderboards ranking teams by usage. 

They made burning tokens a competition. A sport.

It worked. Adoption went from 32% to 84%. Engineers loved it. They used it for everything. 

They stopped thinking about whether a task needed AI. They just used it. For everything. Always.

And that's when the budget died.

The tool was so good that people stopped being selective about when to use it. 

And the moment you stop being selective, the cost goes exponential. 

Because token-based pricing means every thoughtless query costs real money.

This is the part nobody wants to name:

AI doesn't have a cost problem. It has an addiction architecture.

Flat-rate software trained an entire generation to use tools without thinking about cost. 

Now AI billing is per-use. But the habit of "just use it for everything" didn't update with the billing model.

Uber built a leaderboard that rewarded maximum consumption of a product billed per unit consumed.

Then acted surprised when the bill arrived.

Microsoft's engineers unanimously wanted to keep Claude Code. Finance killed it. The people using the tool said it was the best thing that ever happened to their workflow. 

The people paying for the tool said they couldn't afford how much the users loved it.

We built something so useful that the only way to sustain it is to stop people from using it freely.

And that contradiction isn't a bug in the business model. It IS the business model. 

It's how every AI company makes money: build something addictive, bill by consumption, and wait.

tweets.full_text:
🚨 THE AI COST CRISIS HAS STARTED.  

Microsoft reportedly told engineers to stop using Claude Code because AI bills were exploding, while Uber says its entire yearly AI budget was already destroyed by April.

Microsoft gave thousands of engineers access to Claude Code in December and encouraged them to use it. adoption exploded. then the invoices arrived. 

at scale across the Experiences & Devices division, the token-based bills got so large that Microsoft issued an internal order to cancel nearly all Claude Code licenses by June 30 and force everyone onto GitHub Copilot CLI instead.

the company that invested $5 billion in Anthropic just told its own people to stop using Anthropic's product because it costs too much.

Uber's story is worse... their CTO told The Information the full-year AI budget was "blown away already" by April. 84% of their 5,000 engineers were using Claude Code, with 70% of committed code coming from AI. 

heavy users burned $500 to $2,000 per month each. the CTO himself spent $1,200 in a single two-hour demo. they had built internal leaderboards gamifying AI usage. they gamified the spending and then ran out of money.

Nvidia's own VP of applied deep learning told Axios: "for my team, the cost of compute is far beyond the costs of the employees." a VP at the company that sells the chips saying AI costs more than humans.

every CEO for the past two years has said AI will reduce headcount and cut costs. the stock market rewarded every company that said it. the companies deploying AI at scale are discovering the opposite. the more employees use AI, the higher the bill.

Meta built an internal dashboard called "Claudeonomics" to track AI spend per employee. Amazon pushed engineers to "tokenmaxx," their internal term for consuming as many tokens as possible.

$725 billion in AI infrastructure spending this year across Big Tech. and the first companies to deploy at scale are already pulling back because the economics don't work yet...

user.name: Algomizer | LLM Optimization
user.description: We help businesses achieve top rankings in AI search results through LLM optimization services

quoted_status.full_text:
Microsoft just banned its own engineers from using AI.

The tool was literally costing MORE than the humans it was supposed to replace.

They lied to you about AI adoption and now the whole narrative is blowing up:

Microsoft gave thousands of engineers access to Claude Code six months ago and encouraged them to use it. 

Engineers loved it and adoption exploded. But then the invoices arrived. 

Token-based pricing means every query, every code review, every debugging session costs money. At scale across 100,000 engineers, the numbers became so large that Microsoft issued an internal order to cancel nearly all Claude Code licenses by end of June and force everyone onto their own cheaper tool instead.

The company that invested $5 billion in Anthropic just told its own people to stop using Anthropic's product because it costs too much.

Uber's story is even worse... 

Their CTO Praveen Neppalli Naga told The Information that the budget he planned for the full year was "blown away already" by April. 

Uber had rolled out Claude Code in December 2025. By March, 84% of their 5,000 engineers were using it with 70% of all committed code coming from AI systems. 

Heavy users were burning $500 to $2,000 per month each. Naga himself spent $1,200 in a single two-hour demo session. 

The company had even built internal leaderboards ranking engineers by how much AI they used. They literally gamified the spending and then ran out of money.

Now look at what Nvidia's own VP of applied deep learning Bryan Catanzaro said to Axios last month. Direct quote: 

"For my team, the cost of compute is far beyond the costs of the employees." 

This is a VP at the company that SELLS the chips saying that using AI is more expensive than paying humans.

Think about what this means for the entire AI narrative.

Every CEO on every earnings call for the past two years has said the same thing: 

AI will make us more efficient, reduce headcount, and cut costs. 

The stock market rewarded every company that said it. 

Fired workers, stock goes up. Announced AI adoption, stock goes up.

But the actual companies deploying AI at scale are discovering the math doesn't work. The MORE employees use AI, the HIGHER the bill. 

Goldman Sachs forecasts a 24x increase in token consumption by 2030 as companies adopt AI agents. Gartner just published a report showing that even though individual token prices will drop 90% by 2030, total enterprise AI costs will go UP because agents consume exponentially more tokens per task than basic tools.

Meta built an internal dashboard called "Claudeonomics" to track which employees use the most AI. Amazon started pushing engineers to "tokenmaxx," their internal term for consuming as many AI tokens as possible. 

Both companies are spending hundreds of billions on AI infrastructure this year alone.

And Microsoft, the company that bet its entire future on AI, just told 100,000 engineers to stop using the tool they liked best because the per-token bills got out of control.

The companies building AI are telling investors it saves money. The companies using AI are finding out it costs more than the humans it was supposed to replace. And even the company that makes the chips just admitted it through its own VP.

This is the gap nobody on Wall Street is pricing in. 

$725 billion in AI infrastructure spending this year across Big Tech. And the first companies to actually deploy these tools at scale are already pulling back because the economics don't work.

What do you think?

tweets.full_text: Claude code doesn’t have a clock - so that’s partly why. If you keep a session open it thinks your still at it from the last time  you interacted . It also makes inferences based on how you’re typing, so if it’s morning and you’re more Awake it assumes it might still be early because your typing is cleaner but as it get later you get tired and writing gets sloppier it assumes it’s later at night. It also looks for clues like you saying words “tonight”

tweets.full_text:
Testing your agent for resilience is now simple:

1. Install AgentBreak: `npx skills add mnvsk97/agentbreak -y`
2. Open Claude Code
3. Type: “test my agent for resilience”

That’s it.

AgentBreak audits your code, simulates LLM/MCP/tool failures, checks guardrails, and gives you a resilience report with fixes.

user.name: Walid
user.description:
Building AI agent workflows that ship
Claude Code • Codex • Automation.

tweets.full_text:
I've been jamming in Claude Code all week and it's honestly blowing my mind how much of a general purpose operating system it is. Especially with the right MCP servers, it really feels like a Swiss Army Knife. Here's an example:

I was setting up a demo environment for a video I'm recording, which needed some modifications to some Salesforce data. Claude Code flagged this and, instead of going into Salesforce and making the modifications myself, I invoked the @retool MCP and let it go into my existing SFDC connection and make the updates.

I never left the tool I was working in, got some other things done in the background and let Retool handle all the permissioning and guardrails around updating that data.

If you think these vibe coding tools can't do real enterprise work, it might be time for another look!

tweets.full_text:
When you use codex/Claude Code as an operating environment, you don't need code, almost none of it

And you can do everything with knowledge - legal, compliance, medical, finance

And then you give a link to the repository and anyone can just paste the link and use the skill (or program, I like to call it "machine" because it's pretty complicated) and no API needed

People just use it inside their Claude Code and Codex, using their own tokens from their plan

tweets.full_text:
Claude Code gets risky when a tool call can quietly widen scope: read one folder, touch another, hit a paid API, then make the review look small. I treat MCP tools like prod permissions: named scope, approval rule, and a log I can replay.

#ClaudeCode #AgenticCoding
